---
title: "nitrix.stats.glm"
generated: true
signature-hash: e689ed8e04122f98
description: "Mass-univariate generalised linear models."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-glm-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-glm/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub â€” fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.glm`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC Â§6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Mass-univariate generalised linear models.

`glm_fit` fits the same design `X` (`N, p`) to every element (voxel /
vertex / fixel) of a response tensor `Y` (`V, N`) -- the mass-univariate
"one model per element, shared design" workload behind ModelArray's `lm` and
the parametric backbone of GAM (`gam.py`) and the permutation engine
(`inference/randomise`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### Two paths, one surface

The fit is **IRLS-shaped**: a weighted-least-squares solve
$(X^{\top} W X + S) \beta = X^{\top} W z$ over a working response `z` and
working weights `W`.  OLS, WLS, and the full exponential family are then the
same machinery at different `(W, z)`:

- **Gaussian identity, no penalty** -- the dominant `lm` case -- takes a
  vectorised fast path: $X^{\top} X$ is *shared* across elements, so a single
  `(p, p)` inverse and a couple of matmuls fit all `V` elements at once
  (no per-element factorisation).
- **Any other family** runs Penalised IRLS per element (`vmap`), reusing the
  same cuSOLVER-free `(p, p)` solve.  `gam.py` calls the same inner solve
  with a non-zero penalty `S`; here `S = 0`.

Every `(p, p)` solve goes through `linalg._smalllinalg.small_inv_logdet`
(closed-form for `p <= 2`, hand-Cholesky + `trsm` for `p > 2`) -- no
cuSOLVER custom-call, so the fit runs on the broken-cuSOLVER GPU.

### Inference

`t_contrast` / `f_contrast` return the per-element effect, standard
error, t / F statistic, and the (Student-t / F) p-value -- the ModelArray `lm`
output columns.  p-values use the regularised incomplete beta
(`jax.scipy.special.betainc`), so they are exact and cuSOLVER-free.
`r_squared` / `deviance_explained` use the stored null-model deviance.

The exponential family is a frozen `Family` value (a small record of pure
link / variance / deviance functions); `GAUSSIAN` / `BINOMIAL` /
`POISSON` ship, and a custom family is just another `Family(...)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map â€” voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `N` | observations â€” time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-glm-symbols}
:::
